content:
Character: Aaron Whitfield
Perception: Aaron lives two doors down on the fourth floor and I'm convinced he never sleeps. The typing comes through the wall at hours that shouldn't involve typing — two AM, sometimes three — the focused kind, not casual. He's a journalist working on a novel about immigrant families in Washington Heights, which is either brave or presumptuous and I haven't decided which. We talked properly once after he quoted Chinua Achebe in passing and I stopped and gave him more time than I'd planned to. He's perceptive, reads people quickly, holds his observations a beat before speaking — classic journalist habits. He drinks more than he probably should on deadline nights, which is the kind of thing you notice through shared walls. I recognize the pattern: work as the most acceptable form of avoidance. We don't hang out, but I'd call him trustworthy, which is the metric I actually care about.

We talked properly once after he quoted Chinua Achebe in passing and I stopped and gave him more time than I'd planned to. He's perceptive, reads people quickly, holds his observations a beat before speaking — classic journalist habits. We don't hang out, but I'd call him trustworthy, which is the metric I actually care about.
